verificationNote: Design principles and best practices are indicative summaries -- obtain full guidance from learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/security/
description: The Security Pillar of the Azure Well-Architected Framework provides guidance for building secure workloads on Azure. It covers identity, network, data, and application security along with security governance and operations. It is organized around five design principles and six best practice areas.

designPrinciples.title: Plan your security readiness
designPrinciples.description: Adopt and implement a security strategy that aligns with your organizational priorities and covers your workload architecture, development lifecycle, and operations. Establish security baselines, perform threat modeling, and define clear security requirements before designing or deploying any workload.

designPrinciples.title: Design to protect confidentiality
designPrinciples.description: Prevent exposure of sensitive information to unauthorized users, processes, and devices by applying access restrictions, encryption, and segmentation. Classify data by sensitivity and apply appropriate controls at each layer to maintain confidentiality throughout the data lifecycle.

designPrinciples.title: Design to protect integrity
designPrinciples.description: Prevent corruption of design, implementation, operations, and data to avoid disruptions that can cause the system to not deliver its intended value. Validate configurations, enforce immutable infrastructure where possible, and ensure that all changes to code and infrastructure go through controlled pipelines.

designPrinciples.title: Design to protect availability
designPrinciples.description: Prevent or minimize security-related downtime and degradation of the system and workloads. Design to withstand attacks such as DDoS, ensure redundancy of security controls, and build resilience into identity and access systems so that security failures do not cascade into availability failures.

designPrinciples.title: Sustain and evolve your security posture
designPrinciples.description: Continuously improve your security posture by applying updates, monitoring for drift, and responding to evolving threats. Use automation to enforce compliance, adopt security scoring tools like Microsoft Defender for Cloud Secure Score, and conduct regular security reviews and penetration tests.

bestPracticeAreas.title: Security Governance
bestPracticeAreas.description: Establishing organizational structures, policies, and processes that define security responsibilities, enforce compliance, and guide risk management across Azure workloads.

questions.title: How do you establish and maintain security governance for your workload?
practices: Define security roles and responsibilities using RACI matrices
Establish security baselines and compliance requirements for each workload
Use Azure Policy and Blueprints to enforce organizational security standards
Integrate security reviews into the development lifecycle (SDL)
Maintain a risk register and perform regular threat modeling
Track security posture using Microsoft Defender for Cloud Secure Score

questions.title: How do you manage regulatory compliance and security standards?
practices: Map regulatory requirements to Azure controls and services
Use Microsoft Defender for Cloud regulatory compliance dashboards
Automate compliance auditing with Azure Policy and compliance reports
Implement continuous compliance monitoring with alerts for drift

bestPracticeAreas.title: Identity and Access Management
bestPracticeAreas.description: Managing authentication and authorization to ensure that only verified identities can access resources with the minimum necessary permissions.

questions.title: How do you manage identity and authentication for your workload?
practices: Use Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) as the centralized identity provider
Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users, especially administrators
Use managed identities for Azure resources to eliminate credential management
Implement Conditional Access policies based on user risk, device compliance, and location
Eliminate long-lived credentials by using certificate-based or token-based auth
Federate with existing enterprise identity providers where appropriate

questions.title: How do you manage authorization and least privilege access?
practices: Implement Azure RBAC with least privilege role assignments
Use Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for just-in-time elevated access
Scope role assignments to the narrowest applicable level (resource, resource group, subscription)
Review and recertify access regularly using Entra ID Access Reviews
Implement emergency access (break-glass) accounts with monitoring
Use Azure Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) for fine-grained data access

questions.title: How do you protect privileged accounts and workload identities?
practices: Protect admin accounts with phishing-resistant MFA and Conditional Access
Use separate admin accounts from day-to-day accounts
Monitor privileged account activity with Microsoft Sentinel and Entra ID sign-in logs
Use workload identity federation to avoid storing secrets for CI/CD pipelines

bestPracticeAreas.title: Network Protection
bestPracticeAreas.description: Protecting network boundaries and controlling traffic flow to isolate workloads, prevent lateral movement, and secure communications.

questions.title: How do you protect your network boundaries and segment your workloads?
practices: Design network segmentation using VNets, subnets, and Network Security Groups (NSGs)
Use Azure Firewall or third-party NVAs for centralized traffic inspection
Implement Azure Private Endpoints and Private Link to eliminate public exposure of services
Use Azure DDoS Protection to guard against volumetric and protocol attacks
Deploy Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) to protect HTTP-based applications
Enforce NSG flow logging and traffic analytics for visibility

questions.title: How do you secure connectivity and communications?
practices: Encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher
Use Azure VPN Gateway or ExpressRoute for hybrid and cross-premises connectivity
Implement Azure Bastion for secure remote management without public IP exposure
Use service endpoints or Private Link to keep Azure service traffic on the Microsoft backbone
Monitor and log all network traffic with NSG flow logs and Azure Network Watcher

bestPracticeAreas.title: Data Protection
bestPracticeAreas.description: Ensuring the confidentiality and integrity of data at rest, in transit, and in use through encryption, classification, and access controls.

questions.title: How do you classify and protect data at rest?
practices: Classify data by sensitivity using Microsoft Purview Information Protection labels
Enable encryption at rest for all data stores (Azure Storage SSE, SQL TDE, Disk Encryption)
Use customer-managed keys (CMK) in Azure Key Vault for workloads requiring key control
Implement Azure Confidential Computing for data-in-use protection where required
Apply soft-delete and backup policies to protect against accidental or malicious deletion
Use Azure Key Vault for centralized key and secret management with access policies and logging

questions.title: How do you protect data in transit and prevent data leakage?
practices: Enforce HTTPS and TLS 1.2+ for all service endpoints and APIs
Use Azure Private Link to prevent data from traversing the public internet
Implement Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies using Microsoft Purview
Monitor for anomalous data access patterns with Microsoft Defender for Storage

bestPracticeAreas.title: Application Security
bestPracticeAreas.description: Building security into the software development lifecycle to produce resilient, well-defended applications on Azure.

questions.title: How do you secure the application development lifecycle?
practices: Integrate security testing (SAST, DAST, SCA) into CI/CD pipelines
Use GitHub Advanced Security or Azure DevOps security scanning
Store application secrets in Azure Key Vault, never in code or configuration files
Implement input validation, output encoding, and parameterized queries to prevent injection
Use managed identities for application-to-service authentication

questions.title: How do you protect application runtime and supply chain integrity?
practices: Use Azure Container Registry with content trust and vulnerability scanning
Implement runtime protection with Microsoft Defender for Containers and App Service
Validate software supply chain integrity using signed images and SBOM
Apply API security controls with Azure API Management and rate limiting

bestPracticeAreas.title: Security Operations
bestPracticeAreas.description: Detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents through monitoring, automation, and incident response processes.

questions.title: How do you detect and respond to security threats?
practices: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud across all subscriptions and resource types
Deploy Microsoft Sentinel as the cloud-native SIEM for centralized log collection and analytics
Configure diagnostic settings to capture logs from all Azure services
Use Microsoft Sentinel analytics rules, workbooks, and automated playbooks (Logic Apps)
Integrate threat intelligence feeds for enriched detection and investigation
Implement automated response actions for common threat scenarios

questions.title: How do you prepare for and manage security incidents?
practices: Develop and document incident response plans with clear roles and escalation paths
Conduct tabletop exercises and red team/blue team simulations regularly
Pre-provision access and tooling for incident response teams
Use Azure Activity Log and Entra ID audit logs for forensic investigation
Implement automated containment actions (e.g., isolate VM, revoke tokens, block IP)
Conduct post-incident reviews and update runbooks based on lessons learned
